summary: No-ship: the test can pass without proving the claimed user-scope Claude wiring, and the license-isolation assertion can false-pass on grep errors.

findings.title: User-scope claim is tested through GARRISON override paths, not Claude's actual user config paths
findings.recommendation: Run the install scripts with HOME pointed at the temp sandbox and assert writes under `$HOME/.claude.json` and `$HOME/.claude/settings.json`, or otherwise prove these env override paths are exactly the production paths consumed by direct `claude` and runner-spawned `claude`. As written, a script that only writes `GARRISON_CLAUDE_JSON` / `GARRISON_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PATH` can pass while direct Claude would never read the installed config.

findings.title: License-isolation grep swallows operational errors and can mask real matches
findings.recommendation: Distinguish grep exit code 1 from errors. If grep exits 2 or throws for missing paths such as `packages`, fail the test and include stderr/stdout. Otherwise a missing searched directory or grep failure can throw, enter the catch, clear `hits`, and pass even if matches were found before the error.
